title: blechnum neohollandicum
intro: Blechnum neohollandicum, also known as the New Holland fern, is a species of fern native to New South Wales in Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Blechnum neohollandicum are numerous, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the fronds in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the fronds for use in capsules or tinctures.
